r ranges from:

Q3

r = 1 indicates:

Q4

r = −1 indicates:

Q5

r = 0 most likely indicates:

Q6

Which of the following is TRUE about correlation r?

Q7

Correlation does NOT imply:

Q8

If r = 0.9, the linear association is:

Q9

If r = −0.2, the association is:

Q10

The square of r (i.e., r²) is the same as:

Q1

Which correlation indicates the STRONGEST linear association?

Q2

Correlation is symmetric: cor(x, y) = ___.

Q3

Adding a constant to every x value ___ the correlation.

Q4

Multiplying every x value by a positive constant ___ the correlation.

Q5

Outliers can ___ the correlation.

Q6

The formula for r involves:

Q7

If r = 0.5, what is R² for a simple linear regression?

Q8

Pearson's r measures ___ association.

Q9

Which of the following would give r close to 0 even with a strong relationship?

Q10

Correlation r does not change when you:

Q1

Which correlation value indicates the weakest linear relationship?

Q2

r = 0.4 between study hours and exam scores. What does this mean?

Q3

r and the slope b of the regression line always have the same ___.

Q4

Which statement is FALSE about correlation?

Q5

Spearman's rank correlation is used instead of Pearson's r when:

Q6

r = −0.95. This means:

Q7

Two variables can have r ≈ 0 but still have a strong ___.

Q8

Which is the correct formula for Pearson's r?

Q9

If x and y are both standardized (mean 0, SD 1), then b (slope) = ___.

Q10

Correlation between ice cream sales and drowning rates is positive. This is likely due to:

Q1

Which r value represents the strongest association?

Q2

The correlation between height in cm and height in inches is:

Q3

If r > 0, as x increases, y tends to:

Q4

r² = 0.49 means the regression model explains ___% of variance in y.

Q5

Which statement about r is TRUE?

Q6

If we reverse x and y in the correlation formula, r ___.

Q7

Adding a strong outlier that is far from the trend can make |r|:

Q8

Correlation is most meaningful when the scatterplot shows:

Q9

r = 0.7 between x and y. If we square both x and y, r between x² and y² is:

Q10

Which is a limitation of Pearson's r?

Q1

r = 0 means the regression slope is:

Q2

Which of the following could produce r ≈ 0?

Q3

If we double all x values and triple all y values, r will:

Q4

Which is the correct interpretation of r = 0.65?

Q5

Pearson's r is appropriate for which type of variables?

Q6

If r = −0.5, what is r²?

Q7

The correlation between x and (x + 10) is:

Q8

Removing an outlier that is IN LINE WITH the overall trend (not deviating) would:

Q9

Which of these associations is strongest?

Q10

'Correlation without causation' is best illustrated by which example?

📖 Glossary — Linear Regression & Correlation

日英用語集 / Japanese–English Term Reference for M6

日本語 / 日英 (Term)English Term定義 / Definition
回帰 / Regression regression ある変数（x）から別の変数（y）を予測する統計的手法。/ A statistical method to predict one variable (y) from another (x).
説明変数 / Explanatory variable explanatory variable (x) 予測に使う変数。独立変数とも呼ぶ。/ The predictor variable used to explain or predict y. Also called independent variable.
目的変数 / Response variable response variable (y) 予測される変数。従属変数とも呼ぶ。/ The outcome variable being predicted. Also called dependent variable.
散布図 / Scatterplot scatterplot 2つの量的変数の関係を点で表したグラフ。/ A graph that shows the relationship between two quantitative variables as points.
線形回帰 / Linear regression linear regression 直線でxとyの関係をモデル化する手法。/ A method to model the relationship between x and y using a straight line.
最小二乗回帰直線 / LSRL Least Squares Regression Line (LSRL) 残差の二乗和を最小にする最も当てはまりの良い直線。/ The line that minimizes the sum of squared residuals; the 'best fit' line.
傾き / Slope slope (b) xが1単位増えるごとにŷがどれだけ変化するか。/ How much ŷ changes for each 1-unit increase in x.
切片 / Intercept intercept (a) x = 0のときのŷの予測値。/ The predicted value of ŷ when x = 0.
残差 / Residual residual (e = y − ŷ) 観測値と予測値の差。e = y − ŷ。/ The difference between observed and predicted y. Positive = underprediction.
残差二乗和 / SSE Sum of Squared Errors (SSE) 全残差の二乗の合計。最小二乗法で最小化される値。/ Sum of squared residuals; minimized by the LSRL.
決定係数 / R-squared (R²) R-squared (R²) 回帰モデルがyの変動をどれだけ説明しているか（0〜1）。/ Proportion of variability in y explained by the regression (0 to 1).
相関係数 / Correlation coefficient Pearson's r 2変数の線形関係の強さと方向を示す値（−1〜1）。/ Measures strength and direction of linear association (−1 to 1).
外挿 / Extrapolation extrapolation データの観測範囲外でxからyを予測すること。危険。/ Predicting y for x values outside the observed range. Risky.
残差プロット / Residual plot residual plot 残差をy軸、ŷかxをx軸に取ったグラフ。モデルの適切さを診断。/ A plot of residuals vs. fitted values or x; used to diagnose model fit.
異分散 / Heteroscedasticity heteroscedasticity 残差の分散がxに応じて変化する状態。ファン形状で検出。/ Non-constant variance of residuals; detected as a fan shape in residual plots.
等分散 / Homoscedasticity homoscedasticity 残差の分散が一定である状態。良い線形モデルの条件。/ Constant variance of residuals; a condition for valid linear regression.
推定値 / Fitted value fitted value (ŷ) 回帰式にxを代入して得られる予測値。/ The predicted ŷ value computed from the regression equation.
標準誤差（傾き）/ SE of slope SE(b) 標本ごとの傾きbのばらつきを表す標準誤差。/ Standard error of the sample slope b; measures variability of b across samples.
傾きの信頼区間 / CI for slope confidence interval for β₁ 母集団の真の傾きβ₁の範囲を推定する区間。b ± t*·SE(b)。/ An interval estimating the true population slope β₁; formula: b ± t*·SE(b).
仮説検定（傾き）/ Hypothesis test for slope significance test for β₁ H₀: β₁=0を検定し、線形関係が有意かどうかを判断する。/ Testing H₀: β₁=0 to determine if a significant linear relationship exists.
t統計量 / t-statistic t-statistic 仮説検定に使う検定統計量。傾きの場合 t = b/SE(b)。/ Test statistic; for slope: t = b/SE(b), follows t-distribution with df = n−2.
p値 / p-value p-value H₀が真のとき、観測されたt以上の値が得られる確率。/ The probability of observing a t-statistic as extreme as the sample's, assuming H₀ is true.
ダミー変数 / Dummy variable dummy variable (indicator variable) カテゴリカル変数を0/1でコード化した変数。/ A 0/1 coded variable representing a categorical predictor in regression.
参照カテゴリ / Reference category reference category ダミー変数で0と比較の基準にされるカテゴリ。/ The baseline category coded as 0; other groups are compared to it.
重回帰 / Multiple regression multiple regression 複数の説明変数を使って目的変数を予測する回帰モデル。/ A regression model with two or more predictors to explain the response variable.
調整済みR² / Adjusted R² adjusted R² 説明変数の数を考慮してR²を補正した値。過学習を防ぐ。/ A corrected R² that penalizes adding predictors that don't improve the model.
多重共線性 / Multicollinearity multicollinearity 説明変数どうしが高度に相関している状態。係数の推定が不安定になる。/ High correlation among predictors, leading to unstable coefficient estimates.
偏回帰係数 / Partial slope partial slope 他の説明変数を一定に保ったときの、ある説明変数の回帰係数。/ Slope of one predictor holding all other predictors constant in multiple regression.
LINE条件 / LINE conditions LINE conditions 回帰推測のための条件：線形性・独立性・正規性・等分散性。/ Conditions for regression inference: Linearity, Independence, Normality, Equal variance.
影響点 / Influential point influential point 除外すると回帰直線が大きく変わる点。高レバレッジ＋大きな影響。/ A data point that, if removed, substantially changes the regression line.
レバレッジ / Leverage leverage x方向に外れた点の影響力の指標。/ A measure of how extreme a point's x-value is relative to the other x-values.
内挿 / Interpolation interpolation データの観測範囲内でxからyを予測すること。外挿より安全。/ Predicting y within the observed range of x; safer than extrapolation.
VIF / Variance Inflation Factor VIF (Variance Inflation Factor) 多重共線性の強さを定量化する指標。VIF > 5〜10で問題あり。/ Quantifies multicollinearity; VIF > 5–10 suggests problematic collinearity.
全平方和 / SST SST (Total Sum of Squares) 目的変数yの全変動：Σ(y−ȳ)²。/ Total variability in y: Σ(y − ȳ)².
回帰平方和 / SSR SSR (Regression Sum of Squares) 回帰モデルで説明できた変動部分：SST − SSE。/ The portion of SST explained by the regression: SSR = SST − SSE.
